mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various AI frameworks by providing a standardized platform. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes and potential resource overhead.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Kagenti platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the local bin directory.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on the cluster
steps.description:
This step installs Kagenti using Helm, which simplifies the deployment of applications on Kubernetes.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Adds the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Installs Kagenti in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaling configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> Uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step involves draining nodes if necessary, upgrading the Helm release, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node you want to drain.
> Upgrades the Kagenti release to the specified version.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubelet stopped posting node status'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the node is under heavy load or has insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the resource allocation and consider scaling up the node or adding more nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the image is not available in the specified registry.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that a pod is failing to start repeatedly.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This occurs when the requested CPU resources exceed the available resources on the node.

**Fix:**
Adjust the resource requests in your deployment YAML or add more nodes to the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for additional AI agent frameworks and enhanced security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti' showing all pods in a 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
